commit.message:
Implement theme system (Light / Dark / System default)

Wires the existing Settings > Theme dropdown to actual visual changes:
- Four CoffeeSettings colour accessors (ThemePanelBg, ThemeTextColor,
  ThemeDimTextColor, ThemeOutlineColor) return palette values per theme
- SyncAllWindows() broadcasts MSG_THEME_CHANGED to all open windows
- Custom Draw() methods (ParticleGaugeView, SieveDistView, CalHistView,
  ExtractionBarView, RoastGaugeView, ThumbView) use theme colours for
  backgrounds, outlines, tick marks, and labels
- BTextView tips areas update view/text colour on MSG_THEME_CHANGED and
  in constructors so newly opened windows inherit the active theme
- Coffee-colour gradients and extraction zone colours are unchanged

commit.message:
Add context tokens field to Local mode

Adds a persistent "Context tokens" field in the Local Settings box
(below the Model field, default 32768). At launch, the value is
injected as OLLAMA_NUM_CTX and LM_STUDIO_NUM_CTX so both Ollama and
LM Studio use the configured context window. Values below 32768 are
clamped. The setting is saved and restored across sessions.

Reduces image attack surface by not installing unnecessary recommended
packages alongside gcc and libpq-dev in the backend Docker image.

merge: integrate main (CAE sub-tabs + Railway overhaul) into sanarQubIssuesFIxes

Conflicts resolved:
- backend/Dockerfile: combined non-root user security fix with start.sh entrypoint,
  merged consecutive RUN instructions into one layer
- frontend/components/modules/cae-tab.tsx: took main's rewrite (sub-tabs + useToast),
  our alert() fix was already absorbed
- PROGRESS.md: manual merge, appended Etape 14

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

ci: add type-check script and suppress S1128 for app pages

- Add `type-check` npm script (tsc --noEmit) to frontend/package.json
  to catch type-import regressions like the Phase 8 RequiredFieldsForm/CalendarView incident
- Suppress typescript:S1128 (unused imports) on frontend/app/** in sonar-project.properties
  to avoid false positives on JSX component imports that SonarCloud misidentifies as type-only

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(model-3d): build HEMesh directly at box creation, avoid lossy conversion

The round-trip BoxGeometry → HEMesh → BufferGeometry was corrupting
geometry. Now boxes are created with HEMesh as the source of truth,
and LineTool only attempts face splitting on objects that already
have an HEMesh (no more ensureHEMesh conversion).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(model-3d): use point-in-face lookup instead of stale triangle index

ensureHEMesh() rebuilds the geometry from the HEMesh, which changes all
triangle indices. The targetFaceIndex captured during pickDrawingPlane
was stale by the time finalizeLine used it.

Fix: add findFaceAtPoint() which does a 3D point-on-plane check + 2D
point-in-polygon test to find the correct HE face from the line's start
point, independent of triangle indices.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix crawler pipeline: remove broken partition filter and fix Flyway version

- Remove partition-based URL filtering from CrawlScheduler that silently
  dropped URLs dequeued by the wrong downloader instance
- Remove incompatible flyway-database-postgresql:10.8.1 dependency that
  crashed url-seen (AbstractMethodError with flyway-core 9.x)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat(model-3d): add half-edge mesh kernel with face splitting

Implements a custom half-edge data structure (DCEL) as the topological
foundation for SketchUp-like 3D modeling operations.

Phase 1 - Kernel Core:
- HEMesh class with traversal (faceLoop, faceVertices, adjacentFaces) and
  mutation (addFace, splitFace, splitEdge, removeFace)
- Ear-clipping triangulation for concave polygon faces
- BufferGeometry ↔ HEMesh conversion with vertex welding and coplanar
  triangle merging
- JSON serialization/deserialization

Phase 2 - Integration Bridge:
- ensureHEMesh() lazily upgrades CAD objects to use topology
- syncMeshFromHE() regenerates Three.js geometry after mutations
- buildFaceDataFromHE() provides O(1) face lookup vs O(n) coplanar scan
- heMesh persisted alongside geometry in save/load

Phase 3 - Face Splitting:
- findLineFaceIntersection() detects where lines cross face boundaries
- splitFaceByLineSegment() splits faces into independent polygons
- LineTool attempts topological face split before falling back to thin box

54 unit tests passing (39 kernel + 15 face split).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Remove early hard block — let copy review gate handle Scribe task creation

The hard block was preventing the copy review gate from creating Scribe
copy tasks. Now the auto-link + copy review gate handles the full flow:
link action to task, check reviewed_copy, create Scribe task if missing.

Add Forumline protocol messaging to forum frontends

Forums now respond to forumline:request_auth_state with their current
auth state when embedded in an iframe, and send forumline:ready when
loaded. This allows the Forumline App to properly show/hide the login
banner based on actual auth status.

feat: Add dashboard groups for organizing items into named containers

Implements groupable dashboard with the following features:

Backend:
- New dashboard_groups table with id, name, owner_id, position, col_span
- Migration to add group_id FK column to dashboard_items
- Six new group management routes:
  * POST /api/dashboard/groups - create group
  * PATCH /api/dashboard/groups/reorder - reorder groups
  * PATCH /api/dashboard/groups/:id - update name/col_span
  * DELETE /api/dashboard/groups/:id - delete and ungrouped items
  * PATCH /api/dashboard/items/:id/group - move item to group
  * PATCH /api/dashboard/groups/:id/reorder-items - reorder items within group
- GET /api/dashboard now returns { groups: DashboardGroup[], items: DashboardItem[] }
- Extracted item enrichment logic into buildItem() helper

Frontend:
- New DashboardGroup and DashboardResponse types
- Dashboard API methods: createGroup, updateGroup, deleteGroup, reorderGroups, moveItemToGroup, reorderGroupItems
- Updated useDashboardStore with groups state and 6 new actions
- isOnDashboard() and getDashboardItemId() now search both ungrouped items and group items
- SortableGroup component with nested DnD, rename/col_span edit, delete
- Updated Dashboard component to render groups grid + ungrouped items grid
- Add Group button in edit mode
- CSS styles for .dashboard-groups, .dashboard-group, .dashboard-group-header

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: agent logs, per-agent auth, usage tracking, Redis multi-instance

- Task logs: new task_logs table with Alembic migration, every invocation
  is logged (agent_id, caller_ip, task_id, status, latency, timestamp).
  GET /agents/{id}/logs endpoint with pagination (auth required).
- Per-agent auth: agents can have their own API key (SHA-256 hashed).
  POST /agents/{id}/task checks Bearer token against agent's api_key_hash.
- Usage/billing: GET /agents/{id}/usage with SQL aggregations (total
  invocations, errors, avg latency, last invocation).
- Redis: redis[hiredis] dependency, Redis container in docker-compose,
  Lua-scripted token bucket rate limiter, Redis-backed metrics pipeline.
  Graceful fallback to in-memory when Redis unavailable.
- Landing page: 12 feature cards, dashboard link in nav/footer/CTA.
- CLI: `agentgate logs` and `agentgate usage` commands.
- SDK: get_agent_logs(), get_agent_usage(), send_task(agent_api_key=).
- 65 tests (+12), lint clean.

Session #10 — all 5 tasks completed.

commit.message:
feat(social-care): add HTTP layer, normalize schema, and complete test suite

Implements full HTTP layer (6 controllers, 23 routes), normalizes DB schema
from JSONB blobs to relational tables, and expands test coverage to 135 tests
in 38 suites covering Domain, Application (17 command handlers), and IO layers.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: change credit model to profiles-only (comment pages are free)

Comment page fetching no longer costs credits. Users are only charged
1 credit per profile successfully enriched. This is fairer because:
- Failed jobs that never reach profile enrichment cost nothing
- Re-scrapes with dedup enabled only charge for new profiles
- The deliverable is profiles, not raw comment data

Post Discovery pipeline is unchanged (still charges per page, since
discovered posts are the deliverable there).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Redesign site with hero sections, visual steps grid, and animations

Restructured both robot and humans pages with centered hero sections
featuring tech pills (Ed25519, SHA-256, AWS Nitro, Monotonic Counters),
install command, and CTA buttons. Added 3-column "How it works" step
grid with large numbered steps. CSS entrance animations (fade-up,
draw-line) for hero elements with staggered timing. All existing
content preserved.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add --disallowedTools to invoker to prevent agents from writing files

Agents ignored prompt-level "do not use Write/Edit" instructions because
available tools are treated as the preferred path by the model. Adding
--disallowedTools "Write,Edit,NotebookEdit" to the claude -p invocation
removes these tools entirely, making structured output blocks the only
option. CLAUDE.md updated to document the three-layer enforcement model.

commit.message:
Fix Torrentio 403: scope IP forwarding to session-sensitive addons only; add subs display toggle

- X-Forwarded-For/X-Real-IP now only sent to debrid proxy addons (Sootio,
  StremThru, DMM Cast, TorrentsDB) that need client IP for session continuity.
  Public indexers like Torrentio no longer receive these headers, which were
  triggering Cloudflare WAF blocks on Vercel/AWS IPs.
- Add 'subs' display toggle: hardcoded-subs 💬 indicator is now guarded by
  show.has('subs') and exposed as a toggleable chip in the UI (on by default).

feat(navigate): auto-fill landmass, rivers, organic terrain, denser coastline

Major cartography overhaul for maximum medieval realism:
- Point-in-landmass test: auto-detect all hex positions inside continent
- Auto-fill terrain: generates biome art for ALL empty landmass hexes
  using nearest-neighbor biome assignment (no more empty brown space)
- Ground cover: subtle ink grass strokes + stipple dots across entire
  landmass for hand-drawn parchment texture
- Rivers: 2 winding waterways (north→forest, mountains→swamp) with
  ink-wobble filter for hand-drawn quality
- Mountains: caterpillar-style rounded humps (bezier curves, not
  triangles) with dense diagonal cross-hatching on shadow side
- Forests: organic canopy masses using _organicBlob() irregular
  shapes instead of geometric circles/ellipses
- Coastline: doubled hatching density (5 lines per segment vs 3)
  with opacity variation for natural ink-on-parchment look
- Biome region tints: 30% more visible for stronger color contrast
- Auto-fill at 60% opacity for seamless terrain coverage
- Cave terrain: organic boulders + dark cave mouth hints
- Graveyard terrain: tilted tombstones, crosses, dead trees, mist

commit.message:
security: complete auth hardening, 2FA system, session enforcement

Backend:
- requireAuth: add token_version (tv) to sliding session JWT payload
- auth.js /me: enforce token_version check — new login invalidates old sessions
- twoFactor.js: full rewrite with otpauth (RFC 6238), /verify + /check
  are public, /verify now issues JWT cookie after successful TOTP
- server.js: unmount requireAuth from /api/2fa, add token_version migration
- applications.js: remove chatToken from /chat/:chatToken response,
  add audit logging for status changes and deletes
- invitations.js: add audit logging for create and delete

Frontend (all 4 admin-facing frontends):
- EmployeeAuthContext: add verify2FA(), handle requires2FA response,
  refactor formatUser() helper
- AdminLogin: 2FA step 2 UI with TOTP input, backup code toggle,
  help panel, back button
- SettingsPage: replace 2FA placeholder with full management UI —
  QR code setup, TOTP verification, backup codes display, disable flow
- PortalLogin: add 2FA step 2 with dark theme styling

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: graceful app startup — eliminate FOUC on cold launch (#165)

* feat: graceful app startup — eliminate FOUC on cold launch (#125)

- Add `show: false` + `backgroundColor` to BrowserWindow, show on `ready-to-show`
- Add `<meta name="color-scheme" content="dark">` and inline critical CSS to index.html
- Fade `#root` in with CSS opacity transition once React mounts
- Add tests verifying all three layers (HTML, renderer entry, main process)

Closes #125

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: move fade-in trigger to useEffect in App for correct timing

requestAnimationFrame after ReactDOM.render() fires before React 18
has committed to the DOM. Move the .mounted class addition into a
useEffect + rAF inside App so it fires after the first paint.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: fade in library content after initial load

Toolbar, system tabs, and game grid now fade in together (250ms ease)
when the library finishes loading, instead of popping in abruptly.
Empty library state gets the same treatment. Uses double-rAF to ensure
the browser paints at opacity 0 before the transition triggers.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: show window after renderer signals content is ready

The window was shown on `ready-to-show` which fires before the library
data loads. This caused content to appear abruptly on cold launch
(the CSS fade only worked on refresh). Now the renderer sends an
`app:contentReady` IPC signal once library data loads, and the main
process shows the window only after receiving it (with a 3s fallback).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: skip card entrance animation on first render

On initial mount, every card was marked as 'entering' and played a
350ms slide-up + fade-in animation. This competed with the #root
opacity fade (300ms), creating a double-opacity animation that looked
like visual tearing/hitching. Now first-render cards are 'stable'
(just appear), and the #root opacity transition is the sole entrance
animation. Cards still animate in when added later (filter changes,
new games scanned).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: defer reveal until virtualized grid has laid out cards

For large lists (>100 games), the virtualizer depends on a
ResizeObserver to measure the grid container width before computing
card positions. This fires asynchronously — after the #root opacity
fade already started, causing cards to pop in without any transition.

Added an `onReady` callback to GameLibrary that fires once when the
layout has computed card positions. LibraryView now waits for both
`!loading` AND `gridReady` before revealing #root, ensuring cards are
in the DOM before the fade begins.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: gate onReady on visibleIndices, not just layout positions

The onReady signal was firing when layout computed card positions, but
the virtualizer still had no visible cards because viewportHeight was 0
(its ResizeObserver hadn't fired yet). Cards only render when
visibleIndices is populated, which requires both container width AND
viewport height. Now onReady waits for visibleIndices.length > 0 on
the virtualized path, ensuring cards are actually in the DOM before
the host starts the reveal fade.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: start loading=true so reveal waits for library data

The reveal gate (`shouldReveal = !loading && (gridReady || games.length === 0)`)
was evaluating to true immediately on mount because `loading` started as `false`
and `games` started as `[]` (length 0). This caused #root.mounted to be added
before loadLibrary() even ran, so the opacity fade played on an empty grid and
cards popped in without any transition for large (virtualized) lists.

Also commits three previously-unstaged changes from the prior session:
- index.html: inline theme script for synchronous theme detection
- App.tsx: remove premature fade-in from App (moved to LibraryView)
- design-philosophy.md: add "no content without a transition" rule

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* perf: reduce GPU work during initial reveal fade

Three changes to prevent jank during the #root opacity transition:

1. Reduce virtualizer overscan from 1500px to 200px during reveal.
   The default 1500px renders 150+ cards when only ~30 are visible,
   creating massive GPU compositing overhead. 200px is enough to avoid
   flicker; full overscan resumes after the fade completes.

2. Suppress card CSS transitions during reveal via .game-card-revealing.
   Every card has `transition: scale, translate, transform, box-shadow`
   declared — even at rest the browser must allocate compositing resources
   to track these. Suppressing them during the fade eliminates this cost.

3. Disable pointer events during reveal so no accidental hovers trigger
   scale+shadow transitions while the fade is in flight.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* perf: triple-rAF + will-change for smooth reveal fade

The fade was choppy because mainWindow.show() triggers full window
rasterization on the GPU while the opacity transition was already
trying to animate. Two changes:

1. Use triple-rAF instead of double-rAF before adding .mounted.
   Frame 1: compositor rasterizes the newly-shown window surface.
   Frame 2: first fully-rasterized frame renders at opacity 0.
   Frame 3: safe to start the opacity transition without competing
   with initial rasterization.

2. Add will-change: opacity to #root so the opacity transition runs
   on the compositor thread, independent of main-thread layout work.
   Removed after the transition completes to free GPU memory.

3. Increase fade duration from 300ms to 400ms for a more graceful feel
   that's also more forgiving of any remaining frame drops.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: atomic writes with backup for library and config persistence

saveLibrary() and saveConfig() now write to a .tmp file first, rotate
the existing file to .bak, then rename .tmp into place. This prevents
data loss from partial writes during crashes.

loadLibrary() falls back to the .bak file when the primary is corrupt
or missing, recovering the last known good state instead of starting
from scratch.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: stop deleting games when ROM files are temporarily unreadable

migrateGameIds() and backfillRomHashes() previously deleted games from
the library when their ROM files couldn't be read at startup. This
caused permanent data loss when an external drive was unmounted or a
file was temporarily locked.

Both methods now skip unreadable ROMs with a warning instead of
deleting them. The game keeps its existing data (coverArt, metadata,
favorites, play history) until the ROM becomes readable again.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: removeSystem preserves game data instead of cascading delete

removeSystem() previously deleted all games belonging to the removed
system, permanently destroying cover art references, metadata,
favorites, play history, and save state associations.

Now it only removes the system config entry. Games are intentionally
kept so their enriched data survives. If the user re-adds the system
or rescans, games reappear with all data intact. Individual games can
still be removed via removeGame().

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: auto-reassociate orphaned artwork when games are re-scanned

When a game is removed and later re-scanned (same ROM content = same
SHA-256 ID), the artwork file survives on disk but the new game entry
had no coverArt reference. Now processRomCandidate(), processZipCandidate(),
and addGame() check for existing artwork files on disk and auto-set
the coverArt field, eliminating the need to re-download from
ScreenScraper.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* docs: mark library data protection as completed in development plan

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: animate main window close with fade and shrink

Intercept the close event on the main library window to play a 250ms
fade + shrink animation (reusing the existing animateWindowClose utility)
before allowing the window to close. Window bounds are saved before the
animation starts to avoid persisting the shrunk dimensions.

Skipped during Cmd+Q / app.quit() so quit remains instant.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

- Replace utf-8 with utf8 encoding identifiers (unicorn/text-encoding-identifier-case)
- Add curly braces to single-line if statements (eslint/curly)
- Replace Game[] with Array<Game> (typescript-eslint/array-type)
- Fix undefined contentRevealed → shouldReveal in LibraryView

chore: rename repo references from symphony-claude to phonyhuman

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Move page initialization logic to initPage hook (#165)

* fix: patch three critical bugs in core and plugin-storybook

guard comparisonResult null before accessing
.diffBuffer and before the "in" operator check, which throws a TypeError
on null even though typeof null === "object".

move page.exposeFunction() and
page.on() event-listener registration from execute() (called per story)
into initPage() (called once per page). This eliminates the listener
accumulation memory/correctness bug on pooled pages and removes the
non-atomic check+register race condition on exposeFunction.

* chore: update dependencies across multiple packages

- Bump versions of several dependencies in package.json files, including:
  - @biomejs/biome to ^2.4.6
  - @changesets/cli to ^2.30.0
  - turbo to ^2.8.14
  - @astrojs/starlight to ^0.37.7
  - @fastify/middie to 9.3.1
  - fastify to 5.8.1
  - lucide-react to 0.577.0
  - @storybook/react-vite to ^10.2.16
  - memfs to ^4.56.11
  - tsdown to 0.21.0
  - various other minor updates across packages

- Update package manager version to pnpm@10.30.3
- Adjust @types/node version to 25.3.5 in multiple locations

This update aims to ensure compatibility and leverage improvements in the updated packages.

* changeset

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
Co-authored-by: Tim Richter <tim@timrichter.dev>
